feature_costs.label: Core recording flow (state machine, speech, Claude, actions)
v2 features (history, tray, shortcuts, export, refine)

at_a_glance: You have spent .89 across 14 sessions. Phase 2 was the heaviest investment at .01 across 8 sessions — the core recording flow, Claude integration, and visual design all shipped there. Phase 4 v2 features are well-priced at .27 for React migration plus 7 new features. Costs are healthy relative to output.

session_narratives.2026-04-18-001: The kickoff session — brainstorm, architect, and spec review. Relatively expensive per task but unavoidable; these decisions shaped everything that followed.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-002: Phase 1 foundation — 11 tasks including parallel subagents for the full Electron shell, IPC skeleton, and PATH resolution. Most expensive single session at .30.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-003: F-STATE planning session. Zero code tasks but produced the full spec, plan, and task breakdown for the state machine and UI skeleton.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-004: F-STATE build — 5 tasks completing the full state machine, all 6 panels, CSS tokens, and IPC wiring.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-005: F-FIRST-RUN + F-SPEECH planning. Two feature specs in one session — heavy reading day.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-006: Finishing FRN-004, the last first-run task. Short session, mostly a single-task cleanup.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-007: Design system implementation and 3 bug fixes. The L-sized design task dominated at /bin/zsh.27.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-008: DECISION-004 (recording state moved into main window) plus BUG-004. Architecture refactor was the heavy lift.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-009: Vibrancy, BUG-007, and FEATURE-001 splash screen. Splash screen was the L task at /bin/zsh.25.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-010: Phase 2 review + all 11 review fixes applied in one session. phase-2-review alone consumed /bin/zsh.31.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-011: Final review gate cleared — 0 P0, 0 P1. Lean session; app was already in good shape.
session_narratives.2026-04-19-012: v2 features day — history removal, language removal, and Phase 4 cleanup across 5 tasks.
session_narratives.2026-04-19-013: Minimal startup + cost tracking session. No implementation tasks; cheapest session at /bin/zsh.165.
session_narratives.2026-04-20-014: FEATURE-011 pause/resume — full feature from spec to shipping in one session. vibe-add-feature planning consumed 33% (/bin/zsh.225) of the /bin/zsh.674 total.

recommendations.title: CLAUDE.md caching disabled — high-frequency edits preventing cache hits
recommendations.body: Every session adds or modifies an active feature section in CLAUDE.md, resetting the cache on every turn. With 14 sessions and 0 cache reads recorded, this is costing approximately /bin/zsh.15–0.20/session in avoidable input tokens. Splitting CLAUDE.md into a stable core (architecture rules, never list, naming conventions) and a volatile active-feature overlay would allow the stable portion to cache across the session.

recommendations.title: vibe-add-feature planning consumed 33% of session cost for a 3-task feature
recommendations.body: For FEATURE-011 (3 tasks, S/M sized), the vibe-add-feature skill cost /bin/zsh.225 — one third of the total session. The full spec/plan/tasks kit is valuable for complex features but over-engineered for small targeted additions like pause/resume. For features under 4 tasks with a fully-detailed inline spec already provided, a lightweight inline planning step would be more cost-efficient.

forecast_narrative: The project is ~98% complete — remaining work is distribution prep (notarisation, Sparkle auto-update, landing page) and smoke testing FEATURE-010 and FEATURE-011. These are S-sized tasks; expect /bin/zsh.10–0.25 to finish. Total project cost will land around .00–8.15, well within a lean internal tool budget.
